description: Create Hero, About, and Experience section components.
steps: Step 1: Create src/components/Hero.astro. Full-width section with id='hero', vertically centered content, padding-top to account for fixed header. Large h1 'Ryan Cormack', h2 'Principal Engineer at Motorway', p tagline 'AWS Community Builder | Serverless & Event-Driven Architecture | Speaker & Writer'. Social links row with inline SVG icons for GitHub (https://github.com/ryancormack), Twitter/X (https://twitter.com/ryancormack), LinkedIn (https://www.linkedin.com/in/ryancormack), Medium (https://medium.com/@ryan.cormack). All links target='_blank' rel='noopener noreferrer'. CSS fade-in + slide-up animation using @keyframes. Use the theme CSS variables from BaseLayout.

Step 2: Create src/components/About.astro. Section with id='about', max-width container centered. h2 'About Me'. Three paragraphs: (1) Ryan is a Principal Engineer at Motorway with a unique journey from digital marketing to software engineering, specialising in serverless and event-driven architectures on AWS. (2) As an AWS Community Builder, he shares knowledge through conference talks (including AWS re:Invent), blog posts, and open source contributions. He has contributed to major projects like terraform-provider-aws and aws-lambda-power-tuning. (3) He co-founded MemoryStoria, an online memoirs platform that raised EU funding, demonstrating entrepreneurial drive alongside engineering expertise. Then a skills grid of pill badges: AWS, Serverless, TypeScript, Event-Driven Architecture, CDK, Terraform, Node.js, .NET, Lambda, API Gateway, Swift, Infrastructure as Code. Then a styled blockquote card with the quote: 'Ryan is one of the best engineers I have been working with. He is a combination of knowledge, skills and pragmatism. He is exceptional at keeping things simple and avoid overcomplication.' attributed to Eugene Pavliy.

Step 3: Create src/components/Experience.astro. Section with id='experience'. h2 'Experience'. Two role cards with a timeline visual (vertical line on left with dots). Card 1: 'Principal Engineer' at 'Motorway' (Current). Bullet points: Led the design and delivery of Motorway's first event-driven system, shipping in weeks with a decoupled and isolated architecture; Driving API platform strategy across documentation, SDK generation, WAF configuration, and authentication patterns; Championing engineering best practices and mentoring across the organisation; Working with modern tooling including Smithy, MCP servers, and AI-assisted development. Card 2: 'Serverless Engineer' at 'Moonpig'. Bullet points: Built and maintained serverless services on AWS powering the platform for personalised cards and gifts; Optimised Lambda performance using AWS Lambda Power Tuning for cost and latency improvements; Implemented feature flags on AWS for safe incremental rollouts; Contributed to cloud architecture decisions with Advanced Architecting on AWS certification.

Step 4: Update src/pages/index.astro to import BaseLayout, Header, Hero, About, Experience, and a Footer placeholder. Wrap content in BaseLayout with title 'Ryan Cormack - Principal Engineer'. Include Header, then main element containing Hero, About, Experience sections. Add a simple footer with social links and copyright for now.
Step 5: Run npm run build to verify.
acceptance_criteria: Hero shows name, title, tagline, social links with SVG icons
About section has summary paragraphs, skill pills, recommendation quote
Experience shows two role cards with timeline styling
Index page assembles all components correctly
findings: All four components created (Hero, About, Experience, Footer) plus index.astro updated. Used existing global .container and .section-heading classes from BaseLayout. Each section manages its own padding (5rem 1.5rem, responsive to 3.5rem at 768px) consistent with BaseLayout global section rule. Hero uses 90vh min-height with 80px padding-top for fixed header clearance. Build passes cleanly on Node 22.
